text: The EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) is the world's first comprehensive AI regulatory framework, using a risk-based approach: unacceptable risk (banned), high-risk (strict compliance), limited risk (transparency), minimal risk (no regulation).
The US Executive Order on AI (October 2023) required developers of powerful AI systems to share safety test results with the government, while NIST developed the AI Risk Management Framework.
The Bletchley Declaration (2023, UK AI Safety Summit) was the first international agreement on frontier AI safety, signed by 28 countries including the US, UK, EU, and China.
